explanation: Linalool is a terpene alcohol fragrance compound found naturally in lavender, coriander, rosewood, and hundreds of other botanicals, classified as one of 26 known fragrance allergens required to be disclosed by name on EU cosmetic labels. It is a well-documented contact sensitizer — particularly after oxidation — and can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin with repeated leave-on exposure.

Linum usitatissimum (flaxseed) seed oil — the asterisk indicates organic certification — is one of the richest plant sources of alpha-linolenic acid (ALA, omega-3) with potent anti-inflammatory and barrier-repairing properties. It is well-tolerated by sensitive and reactive skin types, though its very high unsaturated fatty acid content makes it prone to oxidation — freshness and sealed, opaque packaging are important quality indicators.

Lonicera japonica (Japanese honeysuckle) flower extract is a traditional East Asian botanical rich in chlorogenic acid, luteolin, and quercetin with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties and documented broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity. It is well-tolerated and widely used in K-beauty formulas for its combined antioxidant, soothing, and preservation-boosting effects.

Lycium barbarum (goji berry) fruit extract is an adaptogenic botanical rich in zeaxanthin, betaine, polysaccharides, and polyphenol antioxidants with exceptional free-radical-scavenging and skin-protective properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and anti-aging formulas for its high-density antioxidant profile.

Lysine is an essential amino acid and component of the skin's natural moisturizing factor, used in skincare as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent that supports moisture retention and collagen cross-linking. It is biocompatible, non-irritating, and well-tolerated across all skin types.

Macadamia ternifolia (macadamia) seed oil is uniquely rich in palmitoleic acid (omega-7) — a monounsaturated fatty acid found in human sebum that naturally declines with age — making it one of the most skin-biomimetic plant oils available. It is non-comedogenic, exceptionally well-tolerated across all skin types including mature and dry skin, and absorbs readily without greasiness.

Macrocystis pyrifera (giant kelp) extract — the asterisk indicates organic certification — is derived from the world's largest brown algae species and provides fucoidan, alginate polysaccharides, iodine, and antioxidant polyphenols with hydrating, antioxidant, and barrier-supporting properties. It is well-tolerated and used in marine-ingredient and protective formulas for its moisture-binding and antioxidant content.

Madecassic acid is one of the four key triterpenoid actives isolated from Centella asiatica, with documented anti-inflammatory, collagen-stimulating, and wound-healing properties that repair compromised skin barrier and calm redness. It is exceptionally well-tolerated — particularly on sensitive, post-procedure, and reactive skin — and used in barrier-repair and calming formulas for its targeted healing activity.

Madecassoside is the glycoside form of madecassic acid — a key Centella asiatica triterpenoid — with potent anti-inflammatory, barrier-repairing, and collagen-stimulating properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated on even the most reactive and compromised skin, and is considered one of the most evidence-supported botanical actives for barrier repair and inflammation reduction.

Magnesium (typically used as a salt or complex in skincare) acts as a cofactor in enzymatic skin processes, supports barrier function, and has been shown to reduce skin inflammation and improve moisture levels. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in soothing and mineral-balanced formulas for its calming and barrier-supporting properties.

Magnesium gluconate is the magnesium salt of gluconic acid, used in skincare to deliver bioavailable magnesium ions that support skin enzymatic processes, reduce inflammation, and reinforce barrier function. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in soothing and barrier-repair formulas.

Magnesium sulfate (Epsom salt) is used in skincare and bath soaks to deliver magnesium and sulfate ions that support skin calming, though transdermal magnesium absorption in leave-on topical formulas is minimal. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in soothing and mineral-positioned formulas for its anti-inflammatory reputation.

Malpighia glabra (acerola cherry) fruit extract is among the richest botanical sources of natural vitamin C — providing up to 65 times more per weight than oranges — along with bioflavonoids and anthocyanins with antioxidant and brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in vitamin C-boosting and brightening formulas for its dense natural ascorbic acid and polyphenol antioxidant content.

Malt extract (from barley) provides beta-glucan, phenolic antioxidants, B vitamins, and amino acids with hydrating, antioxidant, and skin-conditioning properties. It is well-tolerated and used in nourishing and protective formulas for its combined antioxidant and moisture-supporting content; those with barley or gluten sensitivity should note the botanical source.

Maltodextrin is a partially hydrolyzed starch (typically from corn, wheat, or potato) used in skincare as a mild thickener, film-former, and encapsulation matrix for actives and fragrances. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations, functioning primarily as a texture and delivery modifier.

Malva sylvestris (common mallow) extract is rich in mucilaginous polysaccharides, flavonoids, and anthocyanins with soothing, hydrating, and antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and used in calming and hydrating formulas for its mucilage-based skin-soothing and moisture-retention benefits.

Malva sylvestris (common mallow) flower extract provides mucilaginous polysaccharides and anthocyanin antioxidants with soothing and hydrating properties similar to the whole plant extract. It is well-tolerated and used in calming and protective formulas for its skin-soothing and antioxidant content.

Manganese gluconate is the manganese salt of gluconic acid, used in skincare to deliver trace manganese ions that act as enzyme cofactors in collagen synthesis and superoxide dismutase (antioxidant) pathways. It is well-tolerated and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its role in supporting the skin's own antioxidant and structural repair mechanisms.

Mangifera indica (mango) fruit extract provides polyphenols, mangiferin, vitamins A and C, and beta-carotene with potent antioxidant and skin-brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its dense antioxidant and carotenoid content.

Mangifera indica (mango) fruit extract provides polyphenols, mangiferin, vitamins A and C, and carotenoid antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging and skin-brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its dense antioxidant content.

Mannose is a monosaccharide sugar used in skincare as a humectant and prebiotic-like substrate that nourishes beneficial skin microbiome bacteria. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in hydrating and microbiome-supportive formulas for its moisture-binding and microbiome-nourishing properties.

Maranta arundinacea (arrowroot) powder is a fine plant starch used in cosmetics as an absorbent, texture-enhancing, and anti-caking agent in dry formulas, deodorants, and facial powders. It is considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated across all skin types for its oil-absorbing and skin-smoothing properties.

Matricaria (chamomile) flower extract is rich in alpha-bisabolol, apigenin, and chamazulene with well-documented anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and skin-calming properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and one of the most studied botanical anti-inflammatory actives, used across reactive, sensitive, rosacea-prone, and post-procedure skin for its potent calming and barrier-supporting effects.

Matrixyl is the tradename for palmitoyl pentapeptide-4 (Pal-KTTKS), a signal peptide that mimics a collagen breakdown fragment to stimulate fibroblasts to produce new collagen, elastin, and hyaluronic acid. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and is one of the most clinically studied peptides for improving skin firmness and reducing the appearance of fine lines.

Melaleuca alternifolia (tea tree) leaf oil is a potent antimicrobial essential oil effective against acne-causing bacteria and Malassezia yeast, but with significant and well-documented contact sensitization risk — particularly at concentrations above 5% or when oxidized. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin and should be used with caution in leave-on products.

Melaleuca alternifolia (tea tree) leaf extract provides antimicrobial polyphenols and terpene compounds in a less concentrated form than the essential oil, with lower sensitization risk while retaining antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activity. It is generally well-tolerated at extract concentrations and used in blemish-targeting and purifying formulas.

Melaleuca alternifolia (tea tree) leaf oil is an antimicrobial essential oil effective against acne-causing bacteria and Malassezia yeast. It has documented contact sensitization risk — particularly at higher concentrations or when oxidized — and individuals with reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin should patch-test before use in leave-on products.

Melatonin is a naturally occurring neurohormone and potent antioxidant used in skincare for its ability to scavenge free radicals generated by UV exposure and its role in supporting the skin's circadian repair cycle, which peaks at night. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in evening and anti-aging formulas for its powerful antioxidant and skin-recovery-supporting properties.

Melia azadirachta (neem) bark extract provides nimbin, nimbidin, and polyphenol antioxidants with antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. It is well-tolerated at extract concentrations and used in purifying and soothing formulas for its broad-spectrum antimicrobial and antioxidant content.

Melia azadirachta (neem) flower extract provides polyphenol antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds from the neem flower with free-radical-scavenging and skin-soothing properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and purifying formulas for its antioxidant content.
